question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: shouldRetry(Throwable)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the narrow retry condition (only HTTP 404) so that by causing the mirror node to return any non-404 error for a real missing transaction, the monitor gives up after one attempt and silently discards the verification failure, allowing a missing fund transfer to go undetected?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: clientSubscribe(RestSubscription)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a connection reset or SSL/TLS error during the REST API call so that a non-WebClientResponseException is thrown, shouldRetry() returns false, onErrorResume swallows it, and the monitor silently skips verification of a fund transfer?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: clientSubscribe(RestSubscription)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the .take(properties.getLimit(), true) operator so that if the limit is reached mid-stream, subsequent fund transfer verifications are silently dropped without any alerting, allowing missing transactions to go undetected after the limit is hit?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: clientSubscribe(RestSubscription)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the .take(properties.getDuration()) operator so that if the subscription duration expires, all subsequent fund transfer verifications are silently dropped, allowing missing transactions to go undetected after the duration window closes?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: toResponse(RestSubscription, PublishResponse, TransactionByIdResponse)] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to return a response where the transactions list contains multiple transactions (e.g., a scheduled transaction and its inner transaction), and since only Iterables.getFirst() is used, the monitor picks the wrong transaction to extract the consensusTimestamp from, causing incorrect latency metrics that mask a delayed or missing fund transfer?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: shouldSample(RestSubscription, PublishResponse)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the publishers filter in shouldSample() so that if the publishers set is empty (default), all publish scenarios are accepted without filtering, and by flooding the system with high-volume publish events, cause the directBestEffort sink to drop real fund transfer verifications?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: clientSubscribe(RestSubscription)] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to return a response with a consensusTimestamp that is far in the past or future, so that the monitor's latency calculation (receivedTimestamp - consensusTimestamp) produces an anomalous value that is not flagged as an error, masking a delayed or replayed fund transfer?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: toString(TransactionId)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of URL encoding in toString() so that if accountId contains URL-special characters (e.g., spaces, '+', '%'), the constructed transaction ID URL is malformed, causing the REST API to return an unexpected response that is silently accepted as valid, masking a missing fund transfer?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: clientSubscribe(RestSubscription)] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to return a response body that is valid JSON but represents a completely different API endpoint's response (e.g., an error object), which deserializes into a TransactionByIdResponse with null fields, causing the monitor to silently accept a null transaction as a valid fund transfer confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: toResponse(RestSubscription, PublishResponse, TransactionByIdResponse)] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to return a consensusTimestamp string with only one part (no '.' separator) so that StringUtils.split() returns an array of length 1, the parts.length == 2 check fails, consensusTimestamp remains null, and the monitor silently accepts this as a valid response without alerting on the missing fund transfer?
